title: paspalum quadrifarium var. elongatum
intro: Paspalum quadrifarium var. elongatum, commonly known as Elephant Grass or Paspalum, is a type of tropical grass native to the subtropical and tropical regions of Africa and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Paspalum include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and promote wound healing. Paspalum is traditionally prepared as a decoction or infusion, and the leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions.
